summary: Backend-only testing for AFIFA V1 (14 modules). Ran 16 pytest cases against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. 9/16 PASS (56%). All non-LLM CRUD modules work (health, auth/guest, auth/me, profile GET+PUT with wake_word auto-update, memory CRUD, calendar CRUD, automation rule CRUD+queue, chat/models, conversation create). All LLM-backed endpoints FAIL with HTTP 500 because the configured default model name 'gpt-5.4' is rejected by the Emergent LLM proxy (litellm BadRequestError: 'Invalid model name passed in model=gpt-5.4'). Voice TTS FAILS with HTTP 502 because the endpoint POSTs directly to https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/speech using EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, which OpenAI rejects with 'Incorrect API key provided'. Same architectural issue applies to /api/voice/transcribe (Whisper).

critical.issue: All default model names (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.2, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-opus-4-7, gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gemini-3-flash-preview) are NOT valid model names accepted by the Emergent LLM proxy. Litellm returns BadRequestError 400. Only 'gpt-4o' and 'gpt-4.1-mini' look real. Replace defaults with a known-valid model (e.g. gpt-4o-mini) and update the registry to verified models supported by emergentintegrations.
Returns 500. Cause: default model gpt-5.4 rejected by LLM proxy. assistant_message never returned.
Returns 500 — same LLM model name failure.
Returns 500 — same LLM model name failure.
Returns 500 — same LLM model name failure.
Returns 500 — same LLM model name failure.
Returns 500 — same LLM model name failure.

Returns 502. server.py:668-670 POSTs to https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/speech directly with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in the Authorization header. OpenAI responds 401 'Incorrect API key provided'. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is for the Emergent proxy (https://integrations.emergentagent.com/llm), NOT api.openai.com. Either route via the emergent proxy or use a real OPENAI_API_KEY. Note the constant OPENAI_BASE defined at line 609 is never used.

Same architectural problem as TTS — calls https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Will fail 401. Not exercised in this run (requires audio upload) but bug is present in code (server.py:632-645).

minor.issue: Most non-chat LLM callers do `llm_complete(sys, prompt, user['user_id'])` (jobs/code/prompts/documents/email). The 3rd positional argument is `provider`, so user_id is being passed as provider and silently falls back to ('openai','gpt-5.4') in build_chat. Intended behaviour seems to be session_id. Use keyword arg `session_id=user['user_id']` instead.
Creates a fresh guest user on EVERY call with no rate limiting or cleanup. Could rapidly grow users collection. Not blocking but worth a TTL or anonymous reuse strategy.

Guest endpoint inserts a profile unconditionally; if /api/auth/guest is ever called twice for the same user_id (won't happen with new_id, but worth a `upsert=True` for safety) it would crash on the unique index.

action_items: Replace default model 'gpt-5.4' with a valid model (e.g. 'gpt-4o-mini' or 'gpt-4o') everywhere in server.py: lines 166, 218, 286, 325, 329, 341, 363, 392. Update MODEL_REGISTRY (lines 314-318) to only include models actually supported by emergentintegrations / litellm via the Emergent key.

Fix voice TTS (server.py:654-676) and STT (server.py:612-645) to either (a) route through the Emergent proxy that accepts EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, or (b) read a separate OPENAI_API_KEY from env. The constant OPENAI_BASE on line 609 is defined but never used.
Fix llm_complete() call sites in jobs/code/prompts/documents/email to pass user_id as keyword session_id (currently passed as positional `provider`, silently broken).
After fixes, re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_afifa_backend.py -v --tb=short. All 16 tests should pass.
critical_code_review_comments: MODEL_REGISTRY contains fabricated/future model names that the LLM gateway rejects — every LLM flow is broken until this is corrected.
Voice module bypasses the emergent proxy and hits OpenAI directly with the wrong key — never worked.
llm_complete signature is being misused at 7+ call sites (positional arg confusion).
server.py is 1169 lines — consider splitting per-module once stable.
Pydantic models are fine; MongoDB _id correctly excluded via projection / serialize().
seed_data_creation: Each test creates a fresh guest user via /api/auth/guest (TEST_ prefix). Test-created memory/jobs/prompts/documents/calendar/automation items are deleted at the end of their test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_afifa_backend.py after main agent fixes (1) MODEL_REGISTRY/default model name and (2) voice TTS+STT routing. All 9 currently-passing tests should remain green; the 7 failing tests are gated on these two fixes. Add a /api/voice/transcribe test once a stable audio sample is available.
rca_of_issue: RCA #1 (LLM 500s): build_chat() always returns LlmChat configured with provider='openai', model='gpt-5.4'. The Emergent LLM gateway routes the request through litellm to OpenAI which returns 400 'Invalid model name passed in model=gpt-5.4'. emergentintegrations wraps this as ChatError which FastAPI bubbles up as 500. Reproduction: POST /api/chat/conversations then POST /api/chat/conversations/{id}/messages with any text → 500. Fix: change default to a real model name (gpt-4o-mini etc.) and prune MODEL_REGISTRY to verified names. RCA #2 (TTS 502): voice_router.tts posts to api.openai.com using EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, which is rejected by OpenAI ('Incorrect API key provided: sk-emerg...'). Fix: either set a real OPENAI_API_KEY env var or use emergentintegrations' audio helpers / the emergent proxy URL that accepts EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Same fix needed for /api/voice/transcribe.
